info.title: ProxDeal API
info.summary: Private market company data API for the DACH region
info.description:
The ProxDeal API provides programmatic access to comprehensive company data across the DACH region (Germany, Austria, Switzerland). Use it to enrich your CRM, power due diligence workflows, or build data-driven applications on top of verified commercial register data. **Base URL:** `https://api.proxdeal.com` **Current version:** v0.1.0 **Docs:** are available at [`/docs`](https://api.proxdeal.com/docs) (Swagger UI) and [`/redoc`](https://api.proxdeal.com/redoc) (ReDoc).


--- ## Authentication All endpoints (except `/health`) require a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header: ``` Authorization: Bearer sk_your_api_key_here ``` API keys are issued per project. Contact us at **api@proxdeal.com** to get started. Each API key is tied to a **project**. Both the key and its parent project must be active for requests to succeed. If either is deactivated, the API returns `401 Unauthorized`.
## Rate Limits Rate limits are enforced at **two levels** — per API key and per project — across **three time windows**: per minute, per day, and per month. The most restrictive applicable limit is always enforced. Every response includes headers showing your current usage against the tightest window: | Header | Description | |---|---| | `X-RateLimit-Limit` | Maximum requests allowed in the current window | | `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | Requests remaining in the current window | | `X-RateLimit-Reset` | Seconds until the current window resets | Exceeding your rate limit returns `429 Too Many Requests` with a `Retry-After` header indicating when you can retry.
## IP Throttling To protect against brute-force attacks, the API also tracks failed authentication attempts per IP address. If an IP exceeds **20 failed auth requests within one minute**, all further requests from that IP are temporarily blocked with `429` and error code `IP_THROTTLED`.
## Common Response Headers Every response includes the following headers: | Header | Description | |---|---| | `X-Request-ID` | Unique identifier for the request (matches `request_id` in the response body) | | `X-RateLimit-Limit` | Maximum requests in the current rate-limit window | | `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | Remaining requests in the current window | | `X-RateLimit-Reset` | Seconds until the window resets |
## Request ID Every request is assigned a unique identifier in the format `req_<12-character-hex>`. This ID appears in both the `X-Request-ID` response header and the `request_id` field in the response body. Include this ID when contacting support to help us investigate issues.
---


# Endpoints


## GET /health — Health Check ``` GET /health ``` Returns the current health status of the API. This endpoint does **not** require authentication. ### Response ```json { "status": "healthy", "service": "proxdeal-api", "version": "0.1.0" } ``` ---


## GET /v0/company/{company_id} — Company Base-Data ``` GET /v0/company/{company_id} ``` Retrieve detailed company information by its unique ProxDeal identifier (`company_id`). The response aggregates data from multiple sources: core registration data, website URLs, geocoded address, AI-generated descriptions, WZ2025 industry classifications, and registered representatives. ### Path Parameters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_id` | string | Yes | The unique ProxDeal company identifier | ### Success Response (200) ```json { "status": "success", "data": { "company_id": "de-4qcggwpkw4dd41qa", "company_legal_name": "Beispiel GmbH", "company_legal_form": "GmbH", "register_number": "HRB 12345", "district_court": "Amtsgericht München", "company_active": true, "register_type": "HRB", "company_purpose": "Development and sale of software solutions.", "founding_date": "2015-03-12", "company_age": 10, "base_url": "beispiel.de", "website_url": "https://www.beispiel.de", "address_line": "Musterstraße 42", "address_city": "München", "address_zipcode": "80331", "address_country": "Deutschland", "country_iso_code": "DE", "address_loc_lat": 48.1351, "address_loc_lng": 11.5820, "address_state": "Bayern", "company_description": "Beispiel GmbH entwickelt und vertreibt Softwarelösungen.", "industry_path": "Technologie > Software", "business_model": "Dienstleister", "industry_wz2025_primary_code": "62.01", "industry_wz2025_primary_label": "Programmierung", "industry_wz2025_all_codes": ["62.01", "62.02"], "industry_wz2025_all_labels": ["Programmierung", "IT-Beratung"], "representatives": [ { "rep_is_natural_person": true, "rep_person_first_name": "Max", "rep_person_last_name": "Mustermann", "rep_person_full_name": "Max Mustermann", "rep_person_birthdate": "1985-06-15", "rep_person_current_age": 40, "rep_person_acad_title": null, "rep_role_label": "Geschäftsführer", "rep_address_city": "München", "rep_address_type": "Wohnort", "rep_signing_authority": "Einzelvertretungsberechtigt", "rep_section181_exempt": "Von den Beschränkungen des § 181 BGB befreit", "rep_company_legal_name": null, "rep_company_register_number": null, "rep_company_district_court": null } ] }, "message": "Company data retrieved successfully", "timestamp": "2026-01-15T10:30:00Z", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` ### Response Fields **Envelope** | Field | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | `status` | string | `"success"`, `"error"`, or `"partial"` | | `data` | object | The company data payload (see below) | | `message` | string | Human-readable status message | | `timestamp` | string (ISO 8601) | UTC timestamp of the response | | `request_id` | string | Unique request identifier (format: `req_<12-char-hex>`) | **Company Data (`data`) — Core Registration** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_id` | string | No | Unique ProxDeal company identifier | | `company_legal_name` | string | No | Official registered legal name | | `company_legal_form` | string | Yes | Legal form (e.g. GmbH, AG, e.K., Ges.m.b.H.) | | `register_number` | string | Yes | Commercial register number | | `district_court` | string | Yes | Responsible district court | | `company_active` | boolean | Yes | Whether the company is currently active | | `register_type` | string | Yes | Register type (e.g. HRB, HRA, FN) | | `company_purpose` | string | Yes | Registered business purpose / description | | `founding_date` | string (date) | Yes | Founding date (ISO 8601 date, e.g. `2015-03-12`) | | `company_age` | integer | Yes | Company age in years | **Company Data (`data`) — URLs** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_description` | string | Yes | AI-generated German company description | | `industry_path` | string | Yes | Industry classification Representatives** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `representatives` | array of objects | Yes | List of company representatives (see below) | Each representative object contains: | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `rep_is_natural_person` | boolean | Yes | Whether the representative is a natural person | | `rep_person_first_name` | string | Yes | First name | | `rep_person_last_name` | string | Yes | Last name | | `rep_person_full_name` | string | Yes | Full name | | `rep_person_birthdate` | string (date) | Yes | Birthdate | | `rep_person_current_age` | integer | Yes | Current age in years | | `rep_person_acad_title` | string | Yes | Academic title (e.g. Dr., Prof.) | | `rep_role_label` | string | Yes | Role (e.g. Geschäftsführer, Prokurist) | | `rep_address_city` | string | Yes | City of the representative | | `rep_address_type` | string | Yes | Address type (e.g. Wohnort, Geschäftsanschrift) | | `rep_signing_authority` | string | Yes | Signing authority (Vertretungsbefugnis) | | `rep_section181_exempt` | string | Yes | §181 BGB exemption status | | `rep_company_legal_name` | string | Yes | Legal name (if representative is a company) | | `rep_company_register_number` | string | Yes | Register number (if representative is a company) | | `rep_company_district_court` | string | Yes | District court (if representative is a company) | ### Error Responses **404 — Company Not Found** ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "COMPANY_NOT_FOUND", "details": "No company found with ID: xx-invalidid" }, "message": "Company not found", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **400 — Invalid Company ID** Returned when the company ID contains invalid characters (e.g. null bytes). ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "INVALID_COMPANY_ID", "details": "Company ID contains invalid characters" }, "message": "Invalid company ID", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **401 — Unauthorized** ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "AUTH_INVALID_KEY", "details": "The provided API key is not valid" }, "message": "Authentication failed", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` ### Supported Methods Only `GET` is supported. Other HTTP methods (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) return `405 Method Not Allowed`. ---


## GET /v0/company/{company_id}/financials — Company Financials ``` GET /v0/company/{company_id}/financials ``` Retrieve the most recent financial report data for a company by its ProxDeal identifier (`company_id`). Financial data is sourced from official filings — the German Bundesanzeiger (Unternehmensregister) and the Austrian Firmenbuch. Each response contains the single most recent report available. ### Path Parameters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_id` | string | Yes | The unique ProxDeal company identifier | ### Success Response (200) ```json { "status": "success", "data": { "company_id": "de-4qcggwpkw4dd41qa", "finreport_doc_title": "Jahresabschluss zum Geschäftsjahr vom 01.01.2023 bis zum 31.12.2023", "finreport_doc_type": "Jahresabschluss", "finreport_doc_id": "ur-doc-123456", "finreport_doc_source": "Unternehmensregister", "finreport_year": 2023, "finreport_date_publish": "2024-06-15", "finreport_date_start": "2023-01-01", "finreport_date_end": "2023-12-31", "is_group_report": false, "is_liquid_report": false, "revenue": 12500000.0, "profit": 1800000.0, "net_income": 1500000.0, "total_assets": 8500000.0, "equity": 3400000.0, "debt": 5100000.0, "liabilities": 5100000.0, "cash_balance": 950000.0, "current_assets": 4200000.0, "fixed_assets": 4300000.0, "receivables": 2100000.0, "provisions": 800000.0, "equity_ratio": 0.4, "debt_ratio": 0.6, "debt_equity_ratio": 1.5, "interest_payments": 120000.0, "interest_income": 15000.0, "tax_payments": 450000.0, "tax_refund": null, "staff_payments": 3200000.0, "amortization": 600000.0, "material_payments": 4500000.0, "employees": 45 }, "message": "Financial data retrieved successfully", "timestamp": "2026-01-15T10:30:00Z", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` ### Response Fields **Envelope** — Same structure as the Company endpoint (see above). **Financials Data (`data`) — Report Metadata** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_id` | string | No | Unique ProxDeal company identifier | | `finreport_doc_title` | string | Yes | Title of the financial report document | | `finreport_doc_type` | string | Yes | Type of the report (e.g. Jahresabschluss) | | `finreport_doc_id` | string | Yes | Unique document identifier | | `finreport_doc_source` | string | Yes | Source of the report (e.g. Unternehmensregister, Firmenbuch) | | `finreport_year` | integer | Yes | Fiscal year of the financial report | | `finreport_date_publish` | string (date) | Yes | Date the report was published | | `finreport_date_start` | string (date) | Yes | Start date of the reporting period | | `finreport_date_end` | string (date) | Yes | End date of the reporting period | **Financials Data (`data`) — Report Flags** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `is_group_report` | boolean | Yes | Consolidated group report (Konzernabschluss) | | `is_liquid_report` | boolean | Yes | Liquidation report | **Financials Data (`data`) — Key Financial Metrics** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `revenue` | number | Yes | Revenue (Umsatzerlöse) | | `profit` | number | Yes | Profit (Bilanzgewinn / Bilanzverlust) | | `net_income` | number | Yes | Net income (Jahresüberschuss / Fehlbetrag) | | `total_assets` | number | Yes | Total assets (Bilanzsumme) | | `equity` | number | Yes | Equity (Eigenkapital) | | `debt` | number | Yes | Debt (Verbindlichkeiten) | | `liabilities` | number | Yes | Total liabilities | | `cash_balance` | number | Yes | Cash and cash equivalents | | `current_assets` | number | Yes | Current assets (Umlaufvermögen) | | `fixed_assets` | number | Yes | Fixed assets (Anlagevermögen) | | `receivables` | number | Yes | Receivables (Forderungen) | | `provisions` | number | Yes | Provisions (Rückstellungen) | **Financials Data (`data`) — Ratios** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `equity_ratio` | number | Yes | Equity ratio (Eigenkapitalquote) | | `debt_ratio` | number | Yes | Debt ratio (Fremdkapitalquote) | | `debt_equity_ratio` | number | Yes | Debt-to-equity ratio | **Financials Data (`data`) — Cost Items** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `interest_payments` | number | Yes | Interest payments (Zinsaufwendungen) | | `interest_income` | number | Yes | Interest income (Zinserträge) | | `tax_payments` | number | Yes | Tax payments (Steueraufwendungen) | | `tax_refund` | number | Yes | Tax refund (Steuererstattungen) | | `staff_payments` | number | Yes | Staff costs (Personalaufwand) | | `amortization` | number | Yes | Depreciation and amortization (Abschreibungen) | | `material_payments` | number | Yes | Material costs (Materialaufwand) | **Financials Data (`data`) — Other** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `employees` | integer | Yes | Number of employees | ### Error Responses **404 — Financial Data Not Found** ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "FINANCIALS_NOT_FOUND", "details": "No financial data found for company ID: xx-invalidid" }, "message": "Financial data not found", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **400 — Invalid Company ID** Same as the Company endpoint (see above). **401 — Unauthorized** Same as the Company endpoint (see above). ### Supported Methods Only `GET` is supported. Other HTTP methods (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) return `405 Method Not Allowed`. ---


## GET /v0/dimensions — Business Model Dimensions by URL ``` GET /v0/dimensions?url={url} ``` Retrieve the 12 business model dimension classifications for a company by its website URL. The URL is normalised to its bare domain before matching — protocol, `www.`, paths, and query parameters are all stripped. This endpoint finds dimensions for **any** URL that has been scraped and classified, including URLs not linked to a ProxDeal company ID (e.g. companies scraped on demand via `POST /v0/website/analyze`). Classifications are computed once and cached — they are not re-run on every request. Use `POST /v0/website/analyze` to trigger a fresh scrape and classification if no data exists yet. ### Query Parameters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `url` | string | Yes | Website URL (any format — normalised to base domain before matching) | ### URL Normalisation | Input | Normalised to | |---|---| | `https://www.example.com/about?ref=1` | `example.com` | | `http://example.com` | `example.com` | | `www.example.com` | `example.com` | | `example.com` | `example.com` | ### Success Response (200) ```json { "status": "success", "data": { "company_id": null, "d1_value_delivery": ["Human_Service"], "d2_value_chain_position": "Aggregator_Orchestrator", "d3_customer_type": ["B2B_SME"], "d4_market_positioning": "Fast_Follower", "d5_revenue_model": ["Commission_TakeRate"], "d6_pricing_mechanism": [], "d7_gtm_motion": ["Sales_Led_Direct"], "d8_expansion_mechanic": ["Repeat_Purchase"], "d9_scaling_engine": "Linear_People_Dependent", "d10_lock_in_mechanism": ["Relationship_Trust_Dependency"], "d11_growth_bottleneck": ["Talent_Acquisition"], "d12_geographic_scope": "National", "quantified_at": "2026-03-26T19:36:39Z" }, "message": "Business model dimensions retrieved successfully", "timestamp": "2026-04-22T10:30:00Z", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` `company_id` is always `null` — no ProxDeal company ID is resolved from a URL lookup. ### Response Fields **Envelope** — Same structure as the Company endpoint (see above). **Dimensions Data (`data`)** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_id` | string | Yes | Always `null` for URL-based lookups | | `d1_value_delivery` | array of strings | Yes | What form does the output take? (multi-select, primary first) | | `d2_value_chain_position` | string | Yes | Where in the value chain does the company sit? (single-select) | | `d3_customer_type` | array of strings | Yes | Who buys? (multi-select) | | `d4_market_positioning` | string | Yes | Competitive role in the market (single-select) | | `d5_revenue_model` | array of strings | Yes | How does money flow in? (multi-select) | | `d6_pricing_mechanism` | array of strings | Yes | How does price scale? (multi-select, 1–2 values) | | `d7_gtm_motion` | array of strings | Yes | How does the company acquire customers? (multi-select, primary first) | | `d8_expansion_mechanic` | array of strings | Yes | How does revenue grow within existing customers? (multi-select, 1–2 values) | | `d9_scaling_engine` | string | Yes | Primary structural advantage at scale (single-select) | | `d10_lock_in_mechanism` | array of strings | Yes | What prevents customers from switching? (multi-select, 1–2 values) | | `d11_growth_bottleneck` | array of strings | Yes | What constrains scaling? (multi-select, 1–2 values) | | `d12_geographic_scope` | string | Yes | Operational geographic footprint (single-select) | | `quantified_at` | string (ISO 8601) | Yes | Timestamp when the classification was last computed | **Dimension Option Values** Each dimension uses a fixed controlled vocabulary. Key values per dimension: *D1 — Value Delivery Type:* `Digital_Software`, `Physical_Hardware`, `Human_Service`, `Asset_Rental`, `DeepTech_RnD_IP`, `Aggregation_Resale`, `Capital_Portfolio` *D2 — Value Chain Position:* `Platform_Infrastructure`, `Enabling_Middleware`, `Application_EndUser`, `Aggregator_Orchestrator`, `LastMile_Fulfillment` *D3 — Customer Type:* `B2G`, `B2B_Enterprise`, `B2B_SME`, `B2C`, `B2B2C`, `B2D_Developer` *D4 — Market Positioning:* `Category_Creator`, `Disruptor_Challenger`, `Fast_Follower`, `Established_Leader`, `Niche_Specialist`, `Local_Regional`, `Commodity_Competitor` *D5 — Revenue Model:* `Subscription_Recurring`, `Usage_Metered`, `Transactional_OneSale`, `Project_SOW`, `Rental_Lease`, `Licensing_Royalties`, `Commission_TakeRate`, `Advertising`, `ManagementFee_AUM` *D6 — Pricing Mechanism:* `Per_Seat_User`, `Tiered_Volume`, `Flat_Rate`, `Per_Unit_Consumption`, `Fixed_Price_Catalog`, `Value_Outcome_Linked`, `Negotiated_Custom` *D7 — Go-to-Market Motion:* `Product_Led_SelfServe`, `Sales_Led_Direct`, `Channel_Partner_Reseller`, `Marketplace_Listing`, `Procurement_RFP`, `Local_WalkIn_Referral`, `Ecommerce_OnlineDirect` *D8 — Expansion Mechanic:* `Seat_User_Expansion`, `Usage_Consumption_Growth`, `Module_CrossSell`, `Price_Escalation`, `Repeat_Purchase`, `Project_Scope_Expansion`, `None_Static` *D9 — Scaling Engine:* `Network_Effects`, `Data_Flywheel`, `Economies_of_Scale`, `Brand_Reputation`, `Capital_Deployment`, `Linear_People_Dependent` *D10 — Lock-in Mechanism:* `Technical_Integration`, `Data_Gravity`, `Contractual_SLA`, `Workflow_Process_Embed`, `Relationship_Trust_Dependency`, `Regulatory_Compliance`, `Low_Easily_Replaceable` *D11 — Growth Bottleneck:* `Talent_Acquisition`, `Capital_CAPEX`, `Regulatory_Approval`, `Market_Size_TAM`, `Technology_Maturity`, `Zero_Friction_Scalable` *D12 — Geographic Scope:* `Hyperlocal`, `Regional`, `National`, `Multi_Country`, `Global` ### Error Responses **404 — Dimensions Not Found** Returned when the URL has not yet been scraped and classified. Use `POST /v0/website/analyze` to trigger scraping. ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "DIMENSIONS_NOT_FOUND", "details": "No dimension data found for URL: example.com" }, "message": "Business model dimension data not found", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **400 — Invalid URL** ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "INVALID_URL", "details": "Cannot resolve a valid domain from: notaurl" }, "message": "Invalid URL", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **401 — Unauthorized** Same as the Company endpoint (see above). ### Supported Methods Only `GET` is supported. Other HTTP methods (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) return `405 Method Not Allowed`. ---


# Lookup Endpoints The two lookup endpoints — **Lookup by Company Name** and **Lookup by URL** — are primarily designed for **autofill / type-ahead** use cases and for resolving a company name or website URL to a ProxDeal company identifier (`company_id`). Once you have a `company_id`, you can retrieve the full company profile via: ``` GET /v0/company/{company_id} ``` Both endpoints return a lightweight list of matching companies (legal name, register data, country) suitable for display in a picker or dropdown. They do not return the full company profile — use the [Get Company](#get-company) endpoint for that. ---


## GET /v0/lookup/company — Lookup Company by Name ``` GET /v0/lookup/company ``` Autocomplete-style company search by name. Returns all companies whose normalised legal name contains the normalised `query` string, enabling partial / prefix matching (e.g. `"holzstudio sahm"` matches `"Holzstudio Sahm GmbH & Co. KG"`). Results are ordered alphabetically by legal name and support offset-based pagination. Optionally filter by country. Use this endpoint to let users type a company name and pick the correct match — then fetch the full profile using the returned `company_id` with `GET /v0/company/{company_id}`. ### Query Parameters | Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description | |---|---|---|---|---| | `query` | string | Yes | — | Company name to search (partial match, normalised before lookup) | | `country_iso_code` | string | No | — | Restrict results to a single country: `AT`, `DE`, or `CH` | | `limit` | integer | No | `20` | Maximum results to return (1–50) | | `offset` | integer | No | `0` | Number of results to skip for pagination | ### Success Response (200) ```json { "status": "success", "data": [ { "company_id": "de-4qcggwpkw4dd41qa", "company_legal_name": "Mustermann Technologie GmbH", "company_legal_form": "GmbH", "register_number": "HRB 12345", "district_court": "Amtsgericht München", "register_type": "HRB", "company_active": true, "country_iso_code": "DE" } ], "total_count": 1, "limit": 20, "offset": 0, "message": "Found 1 matching company for 'Mustermann'", "timestamp": "2026-02-18T10:30:00Z", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` ### Response Fields **Envelope** | Field | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | `status` | string | `"success"` | | `data` | array | List of matching company objects (see below) | | `total_count` | integer | Total matching companies across all pages | | `limit` | integer | Maximum results per page | | `offset` | integer | Current page offset | | `message` | string | Human-readable summary | | `timestamp` | string (ISO 8601) | UTC timestamp of the response | | `request_id` | string | Unique request identifier (format: `req_<12-char-hex>`) | **Company Item (`data[]`)** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_id` | string | No | Unique ProxDeal company identifier — use with `GET /v0/company/{company_id}` | | `company_legal_name` | string | No | Official registered legal name | | `company_legal_form` | string | Yes | Legal form (e.g. GmbH, AG, e.K., Ges.m.b.H.) | | `register_number` | string | Yes | Commercial register number | | `district_court` | string | Yes | Responsible district court | | `register_type` | string | Yes | Register type (e.g. HRB, HRA, FN) | | `company_active` | boolean | Yes | Whether the company is currently active | | `country_iso_code` | string | Yes | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (AT, DE, or CH) | ### Error Responses **400 — Invalid Parameter** Returned when `query` is empty after normalisation or contains invalid characters (e.g. null bytes), or when `country_iso_code` is not one of `AT`, `DE`, `CH`. ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "INVALID_PARAMETER", "details": "Parameter 'query' is empty after normalisation" }, "message": "Invalid parameter", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **401 — Unauthorized** Same as the Company endpoint (see above). **422 — Validation Error** Returned when required parameters are missing or `limit` exceeds 50. ### Supported Methods Only `GET` is supported. Other HTTP methods (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) return `405 Method Not Allowed`. ---


## GET /v0/lookup/url — Lookup Company by URL ``` GET /v0/lookup/url ``` Resolves a website URL to matching company records. The input URL is normalised to its bare domain before matching — protocol, `www.` prefix, paths, and query parameters are all stripped (e.g. `https://www.example.com/about?ref=1` → `example.com`). The normalised domain is then matched against the ProxDeal URL index. Use this endpoint to identify which company owns a given website — ideal for CRM enrichment or browser extensions where you have a URL but need the ProxDeal `company_id` to pull the full company profile via `GET /v0/company/{company_id}`. ### Query Parameters | Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description | |---|---|---|---|---| | `url` | string | Yes | — | Website URL to look up (any format — normalised to base domain before matching) | | `country_iso_code` | string | No | — | Restrict results to a single country: `AT`, `DE`, or `CH` | | `limit` | integer | No | `20` | Maximum results to return (1–50) | | `offset` | integer | No | `0` | Number of results to skip for pagination | ### URL Normalisation The `url` parameter accepts any common URL format and is normalised before matching: | Input | Normalised to | |---|---| | `https://www.example.com/path?q=1` | `example.com` | | `http://example.com` | `example.com` | | `www.example.com` | `example.com` | | `example.com` | `example.com` | ### Success Response (200) ```json { "status": "success", "data": [ { "company_id": "de-4qcggwpkw4dd41qa", "company_legal_name": "Mustermann Technologie GmbH", "company_legal_form": "GmbH", "register_number": "HRB 12345", "district_court": "Amtsgericht München", "register_type": "HRB", "company_active": true, "country_iso_code": "DE" } ], "total_count": 1, "limit": 20, "offset": 0, "message": "Found 1 matching company for domain 'mustermann-tech.de'", "timestamp": "2026-02-18T10:30:00Z", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` ### Response Fields **Envelope** — Same structure as Lookup by Name (see above). **Company Item (`data[]`)** — Same structure as Lookup by Name (see above). ### Error Responses **400 — Invalid Parameter** Returned when `url` contains invalid characters (e.g. null bytes) or cannot be parsed as a valid domain, or when `country_iso_code` is not one of `AT`, `DE`, `CH`. ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "INVALID_PARAMETER", "details": "Parameter 'url' could not be parsed as a valid domain" }, "message": "Invalid parameter", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **401 — Unauthorized** Same as the Company endpoint (see above). **422 — Validation Error** Returned when required parameters are missing or `limit` exceeds 50. ### Supported Methods Only `GET` is supported. Other HTTP methods (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) return `405 Method Not Allowed`. ---


# Search Endpoints (ProxDeal Datafinder) ---


## POST /v0/search/companies — Search Companies ``` POST /v0/search/companies ``` Filter-based company search across the ProxDeal DACH database. Supply a JSON request body with a `filters` object containing at least one non-null filter field. Returns a paginated list of matching companies ordered alphabetically by legal name. Filters across groups are always combined with **AND** logic. Within list fields (e.g. `country_iso_codes`, `company_legal_forms`, `wz2025_codes`) a company matches if it satisfies **any** value in the list (OR). Keyword `positive` filters default to OR unless `positive_match_all: true` is set. Unlike the lookup endpoints (which are designed for identity resolution and autofill), the DataFinder is suited for structured discovery queries — for example, finding active software companies in Bayern with revenue above €1 M. ### Request Body ```json { "filters": { ... }, "limit": 20, "offset": 0 } ``` | Field | Type | Required | Default | Description | |---|---|---|---|---| | `filters` | object | Yes | — | Filter criteria (see filter groups below). At least one filter field must be non-null. | | `limit` | integer | No | `20` | Maximum results to return (1–100) | | `offset` | integer | No | `0` | Number of results to skip for pagination | ### Filter Groups All filter groups are optional, but at least one field across all groups must be set. --- **`filters.legal` — Legal & register filters** | Field | Type | Match | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_legal_name` | string | partial, case-insensitive | Company legal name contains this value | | `register_number` | string | exact | Commercial register number (e.g. `HRB 12345`) | | `district_court` | string | partial, case-insensitive | Responsible district court contains this value | | `register_types` | array of strings | list-OR | Register types to include (e.g. `["HRB", "HRA", "FN"]`) | | `company_legal_forms` | array of strings | list-OR | Legal forms to include (e.g. `["GmbH", "AG", "KG"]`) | | `company_active` | boolean | exact | `true` = active only, `false` = inactive only | | `founding_date_min` | string (date) | `>=` | Minimum founding date, e.g. `"2010-01-01"` | | `founding_date_max` | string (date) | `<=` | Maximum founding date, e.g. `"2022-12-31"` | | `company_age_min` | integer | `>=` | Minimum company age in years | | `company_age_max` | integer | `<=` | Maximum company age in years | --- **`filters.location` — Address & geography filters** | Field | Type | Match | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `country_iso_codes` | array of strings | list-OR | Countries to include: `"AT"`, `"DE"`, `"CH"` | | `address_states` | array of strings | case-insensitive exact, list-OR | Federal states / Bundesländer / cantons | | `address_cities` | array of strings | case-insensitive exact, list-OR | Cities | | `address_zipcode_prefix` | string | prefix | ZIP/postal codes starting with this value (e.g. `"80"` for Munich area) | | `address_zipcode_min` | string | `>=` numeric | Minimum postal code for range filtering (e.g. `"10000"`) | | `address_zipcode_max` | string | `<=` numeric | Maximum postal code for range filtering (e.g. `"19999"`) | | `loc_lat` | float | — | Latitude of the center point for radius search. Requires `loc_lng` and `loc_radius`. Cannot be combined with `loc_input`. | | `loc_lng` | float | — | Longitude of the center point for radius search. Requires `loc_lat` and `loc_radius`. Cannot be combined with `loc_input`. | | `loc_radius` | float | — | Search radius in km. Required when using either `loc_lat`/`loc_lng` or `loc_input`. Must be > 0. | | `loc_input` | string | — | Free-text location string (e.g. `"München"`, `"Stephansplatz Wien"`) — geocoded automatically via Mapbox to derive coordinates. Requires `loc_radius`. Cannot be combined with `loc_lat`/`loc_lng`. Returns `422 GEOCODE_FAILED` if the string cannot be resolved. | --- **`filters.webdata` — Web presence filters** | Field | Type | Match | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `has_website` | boolean | exact | `true` = only companies with a known website, `false` = only without | --- **`filters.financial` — Financial metric filters** Sourced from the most recent filed financial report per company. | Field | Type | Match | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `revenue_min` | float | `>=` | Minimum annual revenue (Umsatzerlöse) in EUR | | `revenue_max` | float | `<=` | Maximum annual revenue in EUR | | `total_assets_min` | float | `>=` | Minimum total assets (Bilanzsumme) in EUR | | `total_assets_max` | float | `<=` | Maximum total assets in EUR | | `profit_min` | float | `>=` | Minimum profit Minimum debt (Verbindlichkeiten) in EUR | | `debt_max` | float | `<=` | Maximum debt in EUR | | `cash_balance_min` | float | `>=` | Minimum cash and equivalents in EUR | | `cash_balance_max` | float | `<=` | Maximum cash and equivalents in EUR | | `provisions_min` | float | `>=` | Minimum provisions (Rückstellungen) in EUR | | `provisions_max` | float | `<=` | Maximum provisions in EUR | | `equity_ratio_min` | float (0–1) | `>=` | Minimum equity ratio (Eigenkapitalquote), e.g. `0.3` for 30% | | `equity_ratio_max` | float (0–1) | `<=` | Maximum equity ratio | | `debt_ratio_min` | float recent financial report | --- **`filters.industry` — Industry classification filters** | Field | Type | Match | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `wz2025_codes` | array of strings | array overlap (OR) | WZ2025 codes — matches companies where any assigned code is in this list (e.g. `["62.01", "62.02"]`) | | `industry_path_contains` | string | partial, case-insensitive | Matches companies whose industry path contains this value (e.g. `"Technologie > Software"`) | | `business_model_contains` | string | partial, case-insensitive | Matches companies whose business model label contains this value | | `type_label_ger` | array of strings | exact, list-OR | Exact business model type labels in German (e.g. `["Dienstleister", "Produzent"]`) | | `employees_class` | array of strings | exact, list-OR | Employee size class codes (e.g. `["1-9", "10-49", "50-249", "250-499", "500+"]`) | --- **`filters.keywords` — Description keyword filters** Applied against AI-generated German company descriptions. | Field | Type | Match | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `positive` | array of strings | case-insensitive substring | At least one (or all, if `positive_match_all` is `true`) must appear in the description | | `positive_match_all` | boolean | — | If `true`, ALL `positive` keywords must appear (AND); defaults to at least one (OR) | | `negative` | array of strings | "cloud"], "positive_match_all": false, "negative": ["insolvenz", "liquidation"] } }, "limit": 20, "offset": 0 } ``` ### Success Response (200) ```json { "status": "success", "data": [ { "company_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` ### Response Fields **Envelope** | Field | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | `status` | string | `"success"` | | `data` | array | List of matching company objects (see # Website Endpoints ---


## POST /v0/website/analyze — Analyze Company Website ``` POST /v0/website/analyze ``` Given any website URL, returns structured data extracted from the company website plus all 12 business model dimension classifications. If the website has already been scraped and classified, cached results are returned immediately. Otherwise, the website is scraped on demand and the AI classification runs before the response is returned. The `source` field in the response indicates which path was taken. > **Response time varies by path:** > - `"cache"` — both scraped data and dimensions already existed → **< 1 second** > - `"fresh_quantify"` — scraped data existed, dimensions freshly generated → **~10–20 seconds** > - `"fresh_scrape"` — website scraped and classified for the first time → **~40–90 seconds** ### Request Body ```json { "url": "https://example.com" } ``` | Field | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `url` | string | Yes | Website URL to analyze. Accepts any common format — protocol, `www.`, paths, and query parameters are all stripped before matching (e.g. `https://www.example.com/about` → `example.com`). | ### Success Response (200) ```json { "status": "success", "data": { "source": "cache", "scraped": { "base_url": "beispiel.de", "scraped_at": "2026-01-10T08:45:00Z", "company_legal_name": "Beispiel GmbH", "address_line": "Musterstraße 42", "address_city": "München", "address_zipcode": "80331", "address_country": "Deutschland", "register_number": "HRB 12345", "district_court": "Amtsgericht München", "contact_email": ["info@beispiel.de"], "contact_phone": ["+49 89 123456"], "vat_id_number": "DE123456789", "offering_description": "Cloud-based HR software for mid-sized companies.", "services_list": ["HR software", "Payroll", "Time tracking"], "products_list": ["BeispielHR Pro", "BeispielHR Starter"], "business_usp_description": "DATEV-certified, German data residency, no-code onboarding.", "business_model_description": "Annual SaaS subscription per employee seat.", "type_serviceprovider": false, "type_manufacturer": false, "type_holding": false, "type_investor": false, "business_clients_description": "SMEs in the DACH region with 50–500 employees.", "business_partner_description": "Backed by XYZ Ventures. No subsidiaries.", "url_profile_linkedin": "https://linkedin.com/company/beispiel-gmbh", "url_profile_x_twitter": null, "url_profile_facebook": null, "url_profile_instagram": null, "url_profile_youtube": null }, "dimensions": { "company_id": null, "d1_value_delivery": ["Digital_Software"], "d2_value_chain_position": "Application_EndUser", "d3_customer_type": ["B2B_SME"], "d4_market_positioning": "Niche_Specialist", "d5_revenue_model": ["Subscription_Recurring"], "d6_pricing_mechanism": ["Per_Seat_User"], "d7_gtm_motion": ["Sales_Led_Direct"], "d8_expansion_mechanic": ["Seat_User_Expansion"], "d9_scaling_engine": "Data_Flywheel", "d10_lock_in_mechanism": ["Technical_Integration"], "d11_growth_bottleneck": ["Talent_Acquisition"], "d12_geographic_scope": "National", "quantified_at": "2026-01-10T08:47:00Z" } }, "message": "Website analysis complete", "timestamp": "2026-04-22T10:30:00Z", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` ### Response Fields **Envelope** — Same structure as the Company endpoint (see above). **Analysis Data (`data`)** | Field | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | `source` | string | How the data was obtained: `"cache"` (both existed), `"fresh_quantify"` (scraped existed, dimensions freshly generated), or `"fresh_scrape"` (website newly scraped and classified) | | `scraped` | object | Structured data extracted from the website (see below) | | `dimensions` | object \| null | 12 business model dimension classifications (null if the website lacked sufficient content for classification — e.g. bot-blocked or content-poor sites) | **Scraped Data (`data.scraped`)** | Field | Type | Nullable | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_url` | string | No | Bare domain of the scraped website | | `scraped_at` | string (ISO 8601) | Yes | Timestamp when the website was scraped | | `company_legal_name` | string | Yes | Legal company name extracted from the website imprint | | `address_line` | string | Yes | Street address | | `address_city` | string | Yes | City | | `address_zipcode` | string | Yes | Postal code | | `address_country` | string | Yes | Country | | `register_number` | string | Yes | Commercial register number | | `district_court` | string | Yes | Responsible district court | | `contact_email` | array of strings | Yes | Contact email addresses | | `contact_phone` | array of strings | Yes | Contact phone numbers | | `vat_id_number` | string | Yes | VAT ID (Umsatzsteuer-ID) | | `offering_description` | string | Yes | Description of products and services offered | | `services_list` | array of strings | Yes | List of services | | `products_list` | array of strings | Yes | List of products | | `business_usp_description` | string | Yes | Unique selling proposition / competitive differentiation | | `business_model_description` | string | Yes | How the company generates revenue | | `type_serviceprovider` | boolean | Yes | Whether the company is a service provider | | `type_manufacturer` | boolean | Yes | Whether the company is a manufacturer | | `type_holding` | boolean | Yes | Whether the company is a holding / investment vehicle | | `type_investor` | boolean | Yes | Whether the company is an investor | | `business_clients_description` | string | Yes | Description of the company's client base | | `business_partner_description` | string | Yes | Description of ownership and partnership structure | | `url_profile_linkedin` | string | Yes | LinkedIn profile URL | | `url_profile_x_twitter` | string | Yes | X / Twitter profile URL | | `url_profile_facebook` | string | Yes | Facebook profile URL | | `url_profile_instagram` | string | Yes | Instagram profile URL | | `url_profile_youtube` | string | Yes | YouTube channel URL | **Dimensions Data (`data.dimensions`)** — Same field structure as `GET /v0/company/{company_id}/dimensions`, with `company_id` always `null` for URL-based lookups (no ProxDeal `company_id` is resolved). ### Error Responses **422 — Invalid URL** Returned when no valid domain can be extracted from the input. ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "INVALID_URL", "details": "Cannot resolve a valid domain from: notaurl" }, "message": "Invalid URL", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **422 — Scrape Failed** Returned when the website was reachable but the scraper encountered an unrecoverable error (e.g. network timeout, TLS failure). ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "SCRAPE_FAILED", "details": "Navigation timeout after 30000ms" }, "message": "Website could not be scraped", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **422 — Insufficient Website Content** Returned when the website was reached and scraped, but the content did not meet the minimum quality threshold for structured extraction. Common causes: bot protection / Cloudflare blocks, login-gated content, or pages with no extractable business information. ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "SCRAPE_INSUFFICIENT", "details": "Website was reached but did not yield sufficient structured data (may be bot-blocked or content-poor)" }, "message": "Insufficient data extracted from website", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` **401 — Unauthorized** Same as the Company endpoint (see above). ### Supported Methods Only `POST` is supported. Other HTTP methods (`GET`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) return `405 Method Not Allowed`. ---


# Error Handling All errors follow a consistent JSON structure: ```json { "status": "error", "error": { "code": "ERROR_CODE", "details": "Human-readable explanation" }, "message": "Short summary", "timestamp": "2026-01-15T10:30:00Z", "request_id": "req_abc123def456" } ``` ## HTTP Status Codes | Status Code | Meaning | |---|---| | `200` | Success | | `400` | Bad request (e.g. invalid characters in input, conflicting parameters) | | `401` | Invalid or missing API key | | `404` | Resource not found | | `405` | Method not allowed | | `422` | Unprocessable entity (Pydantic validation error or geocoding failure) | | `429` | Rate limit exceeded | | `500` | Internal server error | ## Error Codes | Error Code | HTTP Status | Description | |---|---|---| | `COMPANY_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | No company exists with the given ID | | `FINANCIALS_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | No financial data exists for the given company ID | | `DIMENSIONS_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | No dimension data exists for the given URL | | `INVALID_COMPANY_ID` | 400 | The company ID contains invalid characters | | `INVALID_URL` | 400/422 | No valid domain could be extracted from the provided URL | | `SCRAPE_FAILED` | 422 | The website scraper encountered an unrecoverable error | | `SCRAPE_INSUFFICIENT` | 422 | The website was scraped but did not yield enough structured content | | `INVALID_PARAMETER` | 400 | A query parameter is missing, empty after normalisation, or contains invalid characters | | `INVALID_COUNTRY_CODE` | 400 | `country_iso_code` is not one of `AT`, `DE`, `CH` | | `NO_FILTERS` | 400 | At least one search filter must be provided | | `LOC_INPUT_CONFLICT` | 400 | `loc_input` cannot be combined with explicit `loc_lat`/`loc_lng` coordinates | | `LOC_INPUT_MISSING_RADIUS` | 400 | `loc_input` requires `loc_radius` to be set | | `GEOCODE_FAILED` | 422 | Could not resolve the `loc_input` string to geographic coordinates | | `AUTH_INVALID_KEY` | 401 | API key is invalid, inactive, or its project is inactive | | `AUTH_MISSING_CREDENTIALS` | 403 | No Authorization header was provided | | `RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | 429 | API key or project rate limit exceeded | | `IP_THROTTLED` | 429 | Too many failed auth attempts from this IP address | | `INTERNAL_ERROR` | 500 | An unexpected server error occurred | ---

get.description: Returns all company records from `__master_core` whose normalised legal name contains the normalised `query` string. The input is lowercased and stripped of whitespace and punctuation before matching, enabling partial / autocomplete behaviour (e.g. `'holzstudio sahm'` matches `holzstudiosahmgmbh&cokg`). Optionally filter by `country_iso_code` (AT, DE, CH).

get.summary: Look up companies by website URL
get.description: Resolves a website URL to matching company records by normalising the input to its base domain and comparing it against `__master_urls`. The input is stripped of protocol, `www.` prefix, paths and query parameters before matching (e.g. `https://www.example.com/about` → `example.com`). Optionally filter by `country_iso_code` (AT, DE, CH). Returns company records from `__master_core` joined via `sdac_id`.

get.summary: Get direct (Level 1) shareholders of a company
get.description: Returns all direct shareholders of a company as recorded in the commercial register (Unternehmensregister / Firmenbuch). Includes natural persons and corporate shareholders with their ownership percentages and capital contributions.

404.description: No shareholder data found for this company ID.

get.summary: Get ultimate beneficial owners (UBOs) of a company
get.description: Recursively traverses the ownership chain to identify ultimate beneficial owners — the natural persons (or unresolvable entities) at the top of the ownership structure. Traversal is capped at 5 levels deep. If a corporate shareholder cannot be found in the ProxDeal database, it is returned as a terminal node with `chain_resolved=false`.

404.description: No shareholder data found for this company ID.

get.summary: Get the full shareholder ownership tree of a company
get.description: Returns a nested ownership tree rooted at the queried company. Each node represents a shareholder, with its own `shareholders` list. Traversal is capped at 5 levels. Corporate shareholders not found in the ProxDeal database are returned as leaf nodes with `resolved=false`.

404.description: No shareholder data found for this company ID.

post.summary: Identify a company from free text
post.description: Resolves a free-text string to a `sdac_id` in the ProxDeal DACH company database using a two-stage LLM pipeline: field extraction followed by candidate ranking. Returns the top match with a confidence score plus up to 4 alternative candidates. Charged only when a company is successfully resolved (€0.02 per successful call).

post.summary: Submit a batch company identification job
post.description: Accepts a CSV or Excel file (max 1 000 rows, 10 MB). Maps columns to ProxDeal DB fields using one LLM call, then identifies each row's company in a background job. Returns a job_id immediately — poll GET /v0/identify/batch/{job_id} for status and results. Optionally supply column_mapping JSON to skip the LLM mapping step. Charged at €2.00 per submission.

get.summary: Get batch job status and results
get.description: Poll this endpoint after submitting a batch job. Returns status (pending | running | completed | failed) and, when complete, the full list of records enriched with sdac_id, match_score, and match_status.
Return the EUR-cent price for every billable endpoint. No authentication required.
